adversarial_benchmark.note: Adversarially designed to expose failure modes

bias_quantification.interpretation: Gap between author-sampled and adversarial accuracy quantifies selection bias

detailed_results.failure_mode: Non-standard barrier names (__DMB, __WFE)
Struct-based access, loop-based copy, __DSB not __DMB
Inline assembly barriers, ring buffer wrapping
DMA context, hardware write, __DSB barrier
C++ atomics with mixed orderings, struct members, seqlock pattern
detailed_results.provenance: HFT exchange adapter patterns (Optiver tech blog)

detailed_results.failure_mode: Template class, release-acquire pairs across methods, modular arithmetic
Multi-thread coordination, loop, pointer comparison
Array indexing, separate fence call, buffer swapping
Array bulk writes, game-specific types
Warp shuffle intrinsic, no explicit memory operations
detailed_results.provenance: NVIDIA CUDA Programming Guide, Warp Shuffle

detailed_results.failure_mode: Cross-kernel communication, __threadfence, volatile
Complex multi-phase, shared memory, cooperative groups API
Pointer publication via atomic exchange, RCU-like pattern
LSN-based ordering, separate fence, multiple atomics
Linked list traversal, version chain, mixed relaxed/fence
fetch_add, buffer management, state machine context
Volatile struct, inline asm sfence, DMA descriptor
seq_cst atomics, array indexing, nested loops
All relaxed orderings, broken by design on ARM
Seqlock pattern, READ_ONCE, smp_rmb, goto
4-thread pattern, all relaxed, comments as spec
Array access with computed index, release-acquire pair
Plain (non-atomic) variables
Journal commit barrier, memcpy before fence, LSN-based
Multiple atomic pointers, fence + relaxed store combo
Pointer swing via fence+relaxed, COW semantics obscure pattern
Hash table publish, struct init before release store
Seqlock via volatile + inline asm, not C++ atomics
sig_atomic_t, __sync_synchronize, signal context
detailed_results.provenance: POSIX signal handling best practices (Stevens APUE)

detailed_results.failure_mode: Pipe-based signaling, mixed I/O and memory barriers
eventfd I/O mixed with memory fences, fence+relaxed
Bitmask atomics, exchange for consume, bitwise ops
Multi-phase shutdown, volatile + __sync, 3 threads implicit
CAS loop with two orderings, separate push/pop methods
Double CAS, helping mechanism, complex loop
Linked structure, traverse + CAS insert, fence before CAS
Multi-producer spin on tail, CAS + tail update split
Epoch protocol, fence+CAS, thread-local state
Asymmetric push/steal, multiple fences, CAS at steal
detailed_results.provenance: Chase & Lev (2005) Dynamic Circular Work-Stealing Deque

detailed_results.failure_mode: GCC __atomic builtins instead of C++ atomics or C11
rcu_assign_pointer / rcu_dereference macros, kernel alloc
Completion variable as sync, smp_wmb before complete
READ_ONCE/WRITE_ONCE, multi-stage cleanup, RCU interleaved
Per-CPU data, smp_wmb/rmb, generation counter
Inline assembly ldaxr/stxr/stlr, no C atomics at all
RCU list traversal, rcu_dereference in loop, smp_wmb
Inline asm implementing atomics, stlr/ldar instructions
detailed_results.provenance: AArch64 compiler output for C11 atomics

detailed_results.failure_mode: __builtin_memcpy, aligned_alloc, fence + relaxed store
detailed_results.provenance: Compiler codegen for struct publish pattern

detailed_results.failure_mode: Inline asm lock cmpxchgl, manual CAS loop, volatile
Macro-defined barriers, compiler vs hardware distinction
Compiler fence only (x86 TSO), no hardware fence visible
RTOS queue API, ISR context, __DMB after dequeue
DMA descriptor chain, MMIO, __DSB for device ordering
ISR + main context, __disable_irq, __WFE/__SEV, multi-barrier
Multi-CPU boot, WFE/SEV, struct params, popcount
fetch_add + CAS combo, struct member atomics, price logic
detailed_results.provenance: Low-latency matching engine design (Jane Street tech talk)

detailed_results.failure_mode: Array publish, fence+relaxed, sequence validation
fetch_add ticket, acquire spin, release store
Double-buffered quote, rdtsc, version toggle
Volatile warp trick, __syncthreads partial, mixed levels
Persistent thread loop, volatile + __threadfence, warp broadcast
Cooperative groups block.sync(), tiled partition, shared mem
__ballot_sync, atomicAdd, warp-level intrinsics
__threadfence_system, atomicExch as release, atomicAdd as acquire
B-tree split, bulk copy, fence before child publish
CAS loop for read_ts update, timestamp validation, multi-field
Dual-table CAS insert, acq_rel CAS, release value store
detailed_results.provenance: Li et al. (2014) Algorithmic Improvements for Fast Concurrent Cuckoo Hashing

detailed_results.failure_mode: Version-based optimistic locking (seqlock variant), memcpy
detailed_results.provenance: Leis et al. (2016) optimistic lock coupling for B-trees

detailed_results.failure_mode: Bulk produce, fence+relaxed, ring mask arithmetic
smp_store_release/smp_load_acquire, ring queue, kernel-user shared mem
CAS install, struct init before publish, hash table
DMA handoff, dmb ish inline asm, ownership token
Atomic counter for nonce, generator rotation, fence+exchange
Multi-phase state machine, fence+relaxed, crypto buffers
Constant-time compare, volatile accumulator, release result
Two-pointer rotation, exchange + store, secure_zero
detailed_results.provenance: OpenSSL TLS session ticket key management

detailed_results.failure_mode: Dependency counter fetch_sub, CAS push, continuation chain
detailed_results.provenance: Naughty Dog GDC 2015 job system presentation

detailed_results.failure_mode: Free list as lock-free stack, generation counter, CAS
SPSC ring for audio commands, memcpy params, game context
Standard DCL with mutex, acquire/release pair
3-thread chain, plain data + atomic flags

3-thread RWC, seq_cst fence, all-relaxed operations

Control dependency, relaxed atomics, out-of-thin-air

detailed_results.failure_mode: seq_cst fences between relaxed ops, Dekker-like
Two writers one reader, coherence order check
Full seqlock with writer+reader, fence+relaxed combos

detailed_results.failure_mode: HP protect loop, seq_cst fence, list traversal in retire
detailed_results.provenance: Michael (2004) Hazard Pointers: Safe Memory Reclamation

detailed_results.failure_mode: memory_order_consume, pointer chase, rarely used ordering
Mixed plain/atomic accesses, seq_cst on one side only
OpenCL syntax, mem_fence, cross-workgroup SB

detailed_results.failure_mode: seq_cst IRIW, 4-thread, forbidden outcome under SC
Artificial data dep, release-acquire, simple MP
RCU list delete, rcu_assign_pointer for unlink, synchronize_rcu

detailed_results.failure_mode: CAS loop on double, floating point atomic
4-block IRIW, atomicAdd as load, __threadfence, GPU context

detailed_results.failure_mode: READ_ONCE/WRITE_ONCE macros, smp_wmb/rmb, BUG_ON
Exchange-based push, flush + reverse, intrusive list
Inline asm lock xaddl, no C atomics API

detailed_results.failure_mode: C11 _Atomic syntax, futex syscall, signal-safe exchange

detailed_results.failure_mode: mmap + msync, fetch_add for offset, fence before sync
